findings.evidence: `rg` shows the new lemma at `code/TauCeti/.../Measure.lean:111-113`, whose whole proof is `exact integrable_measureT (by fun_prop)`; Mathlib already has `theorem integrable_measureT {f : ℝ → ℝ} (hf : ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc (-1) 1)) : Integrable f measureT` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Trigonometric/Chebyshev/Orthogonality.lean:67-68`.
findings.fix: Remove this declaration and use `Polynomial.Chebyshev.integrable_measureT (by fun_prop)` at call sites, or introduce a genuinely more general compact-support moment lemma that this can specialize from.
findings.issue: `integrable_exp_mul_abs_measureT` is just `Polynomial.Chebyshev.integrable_measureT` applied to the continuous function `fun x => Real.exp (a * |x|)`.

summary: The exponential-moment declaration is only a named specialization of Mathlib's existing Chebyshev compact-support integrability theorem, so this PR duplicates API instead of reusing it.
